perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall rema in the property of Writer and shall not be disclosed or revealed by Producer or anyone else except employees of Producer who have a need to know the information in connection with Producer’s evalua tion of the Submission, and who have entered into a secrecy agreement with Producer under which such employees are required to keep confidential the Confidential Information of Writer, and such employees shall be advised by Producer of the confidential nature of the information and that the information shall be treated accordingly. Producer shall be liable for any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information by its employees.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall rema in the property of Writer and shall not be disclosed or revealed by Producer to external parties, but the Producer can internally share the information with all employees. Producer shall be liable for any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information to external parties by its employees.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirement for employees to have a 'need to know' and secrecy agreements, instead allowing internal sharing with all employees. It also limits liability to disclosures to external parties. This contradicts paragraph 5(i), which states the Producer must not 'directly or indirectly disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without the consent of Writer.' By allowing unrestricted internal sharing, it increases the risk of indirect disclosure to third parties and blurs the line of what constitutes a breach of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: .6. (i) Producer shall notify Writer of any determin ation it may arrive at with respect to the further development of the submission, provided, however, that, in doing so, Producer shall not directly or indirectly disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without the consent of Writer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'need to know' and secrecy agreement requirements for employees, and limits liability to external disclosures. This directly contradicts section 6(i), which prohibits 'directly or indirectly' disclosing confidential information to any third party without the Writer's consent. The unrestricted internal sharing allowed by the changed text significantly increases the risk of indirect disclosure, thus creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If Producer determines that the Submission ca nnot be further developed into a Project, within <….> months of the receipt of the Subm ission, Producer shall within five (5) business days after such determination return any and all Confidential Informati on to Writer, along with all copies or derivatives thereof and all writings generated by Producer in c onnection with Producer’s evaluation of the Submission or the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If Producer determines that the Submission ca nnot be further developed into a Project, within <….> months of the receipt of the Subm ission, Producer shall make reasonable efforts to return Confidential Information to Writer, along with all copies or derivatives thereof and all writings generated by Producer in c onnection with Producer’s evaluation of the Submission or the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall return' to 'shall make reasonable efforts to return', the obligation to return Confidential Information is weakened and becomes ambiguous. This introduces uncertainty about the Producer's responsibility to safeguard the Confidential Information after deciding not to develop the Project, contradicting paragraph 10, which stipulates that the obligation to protect the information survives termination. It isn't clear what constitutes 'reasonable efforts'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: .10. Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3,4, and 8 of this agreement shall extend from the date of this agreement and shall survive the expiration or term ination of this agreement, provided, however, that Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this agreement shall terminate immediately in the event that Writer .shall purposefully disclose the Confidential Information to any other person, firm, or corporation on a non-confidential basis, during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the producer 'shall return' all Confidential Information, which implies a strict obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'shall make reasonable efforts to return,' introducing ambiguity. This directly contradicts paragraph .10, which states that the Producer's obligations under paragraph 8 (which implicitly relates to the return of confidential information) 'shall survive the expiration or termination of this agreement.' The 'reasonable efforts' language undermines the survival clause because it's unclear if reasonable efforts continue after termination, thus creating a direct conflict and in-text contradiction, adhering to the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
